chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُقْتَلُ وَ لَهُ وَلِيَّانِ أَوْ أَكْثَرُ فَيَعْفُو أَحَدُهُمْ أَوْ يَقْبَلُ الدِّيَةَ وَ بَعْضٌ يُرِيدُ الْقَتْلَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 46 – The man is killed and for him are two guardians, or more, so one of them forgives or accepts the wergild, and one wants the (retaliatory) killing

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ قُتِلَ وَ لَهُ أَوْلَادٌ صِغَارٌ وَ كِبَارٌ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ عَفَا الْأَوْلَادُ الْكِبَارُ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَا يُقْتَلُ وَ يَجُوزُ عَفْوُ الْأَوْلَادِ الْكِبَارِ فِي حِصَصِهِمْ فَإِذَا كَبِرَ الصِّغَارُ كَانَ لَهُمْ أَنْ يَطْلُبُوا حِصَصَهُمْ مِنَ الدِّيَةِ .

en.hubeali: Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Wallad who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who was killed and for him were young children, and older ones. What is your<sup>asws</sup> view that if the older children forgive?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He would not be killed, and the forgiveness of the older children is allowed regarding their share. So when the young ones become older, it would be for them to invalidate (forgive) their shares from the wergild’.
